title: oxalis levis
intro: Oxalis levis, also known as the violet woodsorrel, is a perennial plant in the wood sorrel family that is native to South America. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Oxalis levis has been used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
